phase3_findings.description: The composed --agents payload is an effort-only entry keyed by an INSTALLED plugin agent id ({"devflow:code-reviewer":{"effort":"low"}}) with no description/prompt. The spike (agents-seam-probe.yml) proved only a fully-defined NEW agent (seam-probe-agent with description+prompt+effort). Nothing establishes that (a) a definition missing description/prompt is accepted, or (b) an entry keyed to an installed plugin agent PATCHES its effort rather than DEFINING/SHADOWING it. ORCHESTRATOR-CONFIRMED: this repo's own config carries `default: {effort: low}`, so `bash scripts/compose-applied-effort.sh` emits prompt-less definitions for ALL NINE review subagents. If --agents shadows, every merge-gating reviewer degrades to a prompt-less definition on the next cloud run.

Valid-falsy sidecar value coerced into an applied claim. lib/efficiency-trace.jq branches on `$applied != null` only, so a sidecar entry with value "" (or 0, {}, or a non-enum string) is non-null and emits application_point: agent-definition with effective: "" — an unearned applied claim, violating the code's own 'unknown is not zero' contract and AC2. ORCHESTRATOR-CONFIRMED by execution: value "" -> {"application_point":"agent-definition","effective":""}; value {"effort":"low"} -> effective is a DICT; value "turbo" -> effective "turbo". The six-shape matrix is swept only at container level, never per-agent value level.

load_applied_effort's 'fail-CLOSED to {}' contract is false for a valid-JSON-prefix-plus-trailing-garbage sidecar: jq emits the parsed object AND exits 5, so `|| printf '{}'` appends a SECOND document; --argjson then rejects the two-document string and the WHOLE record aborts. ORCHESTRATOR-CONFIRMED by execution: sidecar '{"devflow:code-reviewer":"low"} garbage' -> rc=2, ZERO bytes of record output, 'jq: invalid JSON text passed to --argjson'. Both a documented falsehood and a real availability defect (total telemetry loss). The M2 test only covers a TRUNCATED sidecar (jq emits nothing), which is why the claim reads as covered.

B1 trust boundary is bypassable: the review job checks out the PR HEAD (devflow-runner.yml:159 `ref: ${{ inputs.ref }}`) and the new composer step runs `bash "$CAE"` with a `scripts/compose-applied-effort.sh` fallback and NO vendor_source=='fetch' gate. Both the composer and the resolver are ordinary tracked files a PR author can edit, so a PR can ignore DEVFLOW_AE_CONFIG entirely. This is the exact #402/#404 'a floor the PR controls is no floor' class the SAME workflow already guards against for lib/efficiency-trace.sh via harden-stop-hooks.sh — the new helpers were not added to that hardened set. Consequently three diff-added absolute claims are false against HEAD (changeset 'a PR author can no longer lower...'; composer header 'cannot steer...'; step comment 'NEVER the PR-head working tree' — true of the config, not of the code that reads it).

Self-contradicting diff: lib/test/run.sh:33927-33929 comment says the composer step is 'triplicated scaffolding too (identical env AND run: across the three workflows)', but the SAME PR asserts the opposite twice — run.sh:33896 'only the env differs across the three tiers' and run.sh:33912 pins DEVFLOW_AE_CONFIG count '0,0' on impl/command. The checker itself only compares st['run'], never env. ORCHESTRATOR-CONFIRMED by reading all three sites.

The unconditional stale-sidecar clear lives INSIDE the helper it protects. When neither the vendored nor the self-repo helper path exists (the documented #502 consumer-skew state), the workflow step's `if [ -f "$CAE" ]` is skipped and `rm -f "$SIDECAR"` never runs — so a sidecar present in the checked-out tree is read by the recorder as genuine applied telemetry. On the review tier that tree is the PR HEAD, and .devflow/tmp/ being gitignored is no barrier (`git add -f`). ORCHESTRATOR-CONFIRMED: no workflow references agent-effort-applied.json, so the clear exists only in the helper. This also falsifies the triplicated step comment 'any failure yields an empty agents_args and no sidecar'.

Two B1 security pins are presence-only (assert_pin_red_on_removal) carrying `# structural-pin-ok:` rationales that are factually false: 'no in-place mutation reintroduces PR-head sourcing'. A mutation plainly exists — sed the value ${{ steps.baseprovision.outputs.config_file }} to a head-derived expression, reintroducing the guarded regression while leaving a DEVFLOW_AE_CONFIG: line present. These are behavioral security fixes, so the repo's #666 mutation-routing convention requires assert_pin_red_under. The marker rationale is itself a documented falsehood. ORCHESTRATOR-CONFIRMED by reading run.sh:33900-33908.

compose-applied-effort.sh emits NO stderr breadcrumb on ANY of its five fail-open paths (resolver absent, resolver failure, non-object/empty JSON, mkdir failure, sidecar write failure). This repo's best-effort-helper contract (lib/resolve-bin.sh, scripts/ensure-label.sh, scripts/apply-labels.sh, lib/normalize-path.sh) always pairs 'always exit 0' with a stderr breadcrumb. Fail-open without a breadcrumb is silent failure: an operator cannot distinguish 'the capability gate stripped it' from 'the composer never ran'.

`2>/dev/null` on BOTH resolver invocations discards the resolver's documented config-shape and unknown-agent-drift warnings — the only channel naming the diagnosable cause. The resolver's own comment says it warns 'so it isn't a silent no-op', and this PR's new main() comment asserts 'config-shape warnings still go to stderr above' while this PR simultaneously ships the only production caller and routes that stderr to /dev/null. The warning path is dead in the deployed configuration.

compose-applied-effort.sh header bullet 4 attributes the applied-recorded invariant to 'the split-brain build_applied_effort avoids by returning both from one gate pass', while the deferral note ~80 lines later states 'this is a SECOND, independent resolver process — not one gate pass reused'. Read together in one file they contradict. The absolute 'the two separate calls cannot disagree' also overreaches: two processes each re-read the config from disk, so determinism holds only for an unchanged config.

Applier-recorder default-path coupling is never behaviorally proven. The S2 assertions pin two DIFFERENT literals in two files, each counted only in its own file; nothing asserts the two expressions resolve to the same path. Every recorder test sets DEVFLOW_APPLIED_EFFORT_FILE and every composer test sets DEVFLOW_AE_SIDECAR, so the default branch of both is untested. Compounded by the two env-var names differing (DEVFLOW_AE_SIDECAR vs DEVFLOW_APPLIED_EFFORT_FILE) for one channel.

Sidecar-write-succeeds-but-empty is an uncovered applied-implies-recorded hole: step 4 emits the splice on the second resolver call's EXIT STATUS alone, never validating its content, while call 1's content IS validated. A pass exiting 0 with empty/{} stdout produces a sidecar the recorder parses to {} while --agents was applied — applied-but-unrecorded, the invariant the block exists to hold. Asymmetric guard.

--applied-agents-json and --applied-sidecar-json are silently mutually-preferential (`payload = agents_defs if args.applied_agents_json else sidecar`) rather than mutually exclusive. A caller passing both writes the agent-definition map into the sidecar path; the recorder would then set effective to a dict. Use add_mutually_exclusive_group().

Resolver-path resolution branch uncovered: DEVFLOW_RRO is set in every composer test, so the vendored -> self-repo fallback chain (which decides which resolver runs in a consumer repo) is never exercised. A typo in the vendored literal would ship green.

`rm -f "$SIDECAR"` return value is unchecked; rm -f still returns nonzero on a real removal failure (read-only mount, immutable file). The header states the invariant unconditionally ('a run that applies NOTHING leaves no sidecar'), which is false when the removal fails.

Phase 0.6 stale-prose lint reported an UNRESOLVABLE R3 count-locked row: docs/agents-seam-probe.md:67 'recognition-only (8 files)'. Per this repo's pin-or-don't-write policy an exact-count claim in shipped prose must be bound to a suite pin or reworded drift-proof.

fix_decisions.evidence: AC1 application half deferred behind DEVFLOW_AE_APPLY (default off) per operator decision; AC2 telemetry half ships
trusted-source ladder for the composer step: operator-approved, NOT YET IMPLEMENTED this iteration
stale-sidecar clear still inside the helper; helper-absent path unguarded
B1 pins still presence-only with a false structural-pin-ok rationale
applier/recorder default-path coupling still not behaviorally proven
resolver vendored->self-repo fallback branch still uncovered

rows: UNRESOLVABLE	R3	docs/agents-seam-probe.md	67	count-locked: recognition-only (8 files)
stale_prose_check.note: exit 0, no STALE rows; 1 UNRESOLVABLE (informational, never gates)

phase3_findings.description: Docs assert the applied arm ships and applies on the cloud tier; the shipped composer is inert by default (DEVFLOW_AE_APPLY set by no workflow), so no per-agent effort is applied and agent-definition is never recorded.

The changeset states a configured per-agent effort 'takes effect instead of the honest fallback'. False against HEAD, and consolidate-changesets.py prepends this verbatim into the public CHANGELOG on merge.
compose-applied-effort.sh tells a maintainer to 'restore the claude_args splice' when arming the gate. The splice was never removed - all three workflows carry it at HEAD and run.sh pins it present.
run.sh comment cites the '|| printf {}' fallback form as current; the F3 fix in this same PR replaced it with a capture-then-branch form.
The three triplicated workflow step comments describe the step as composing --agents and writing the sidecar, with no mention of the default-off gate, so an operator reads the step as live.
docs/agents-seam-probe.md records 'Decision: the applied arm SHIPS', contradicting the shipped gate; this is the evidence-of-record the next probe dispatch is adjudicated against.

The workflow's helper-absent branch is silent AND co-gates the stale-sidecar clear: with neither helper path present (the #502 vendor-skew state) no breadcrumb is emitted and a PR-committed sidecar is never cleared, so the recorder reads author-supplied values as genuine applied telemetry.
No test asserts the shipped workflows leave the apply gate off; the deferral of an unmeasured --agents shape was guarded only by an env var's absence.

Every composer test stubs the resolver with space-free JSON while the real producer (json.dumps) emits spaces, so the asserted splice literal is a shape the shipped resolver never produces and the space-bearing claude_args path is unexercised.
$applied_effort is now a required jq arg with no vendor-skew coverage: an old .sh paired with a new .jq yields a compile error and total telemetry loss rather than fail-closed degradation.
The applier/recorder default-path handoff is pinned by two literals but never behaviorally driven.
DEVFLOW_AE_APPLY is forwarded by no workflow env and absent from config.schema.json, so arming the arm is a workflows-scoped code change, not an operator action.
A sidecar naming an agent outside $effort_roster is silently dropped; correct but unasserted.
build_applied_effort docstring implies a live production path without noting the composer that calls it is inert by default.
run.sh's 'gate is opt-in' framing is true of the helper but reads as operator opt-in; no deployed tier sets it.
